title: Define Meshtastic synthetic metadata and firmware version policy
description: Why this exists: Meshtastic Apple clients may fail version checks if DeviceMetadata/firmware_version is absent, but LICHEN must not misrepresent itself as Meshtastic RF firmware. What needs to be done: define synthetic metadata, min app version, hw model/capability fields, and user-visible naming for LICHEN compatibility mode. Acceptance criteria: policy passes Apple/Android/Python discovery expectations, clearly labels LICHEN compatibility, and documents fields that are synthetic or unsupported.
design:
Meshtastic synthetic metadata and firmware version policy, recorded 2026-07-01.

Sources checked:
- Local pinned protobuf baseline: meshtastic/protobufs 032b7dfd68e875c4323e6ac67590c6fc616b1714, recorded in docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md and lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/include/lichen/meshtastic/codec.h.
- Active Zephyr C metadata encoder: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/codec.c.
- Active gateway local-info provider: lichen/apps/gateway/src/meshtastic_adapter.c.
- Current upstream Meshtastic protobuf docs still define DeviceMetadata as the firmware/protobuf/min-app metadata response surface, and current Meshtastic firmware releases are in the 2.7.x series as of 2026-07-01.

Policy:
- Metadata is synthetic and exists only for local Meshtastic app compatibility. It MUST NOT imply Meshtastic RF interoperability or that the node is running Meshtastic RF firmware.
- firmware_version must be visibly LICHEN-branded, e.g. "LICHEN compat <version>" or "LICHEN compat <version>+zephyr.<rtos-version>". It must never be a bare Meshtastic firmware version, must not contain the word Meshtastic in any casing, and must not present an unlabeled RTOS version as the LICHEN firmware release.
- min_app_version defaults to 30200 until Android/iOS/Python smoke testing proves a higher minimum is required.
- pio_env is Zephyr-branded, e.g. "zephyr-r1_neo_nrf52840".
- DeviceMetadata.hw_model and User.hw_model use PRIVATE_HW (255) unless Meshtastic upstream adds an official LICHEN enum. Do not claim T-Beam, Heltec, RAK, or other Meshtastic board enum values even when running on that hardware.
- role is CLIENT unless a later tested app flow requires another local-app role. Router/repeater roles are avoided because they imply Meshtastic RF behavior.
- has_bluetooth is true only when the Meshtastic BLE compatibility surface is active. has_wifi, has_ethernet, has_remote_hardware, and has_pkc are false for the MVP unless backed by implemented LICHEN capabilities. position_flags is zero until HAL location/provider support is wired into the adapter.
- excluded_modules is a bitmask of unsupported Meshtastic modules/features for the MVP. It excludes MQTT, serial module, external notification, store-and-forward, range test, telemetry, canned message, audio, remote hardware, neighbor info, ambient lighting, detection sensor, paxcounter, and network config. Bluetooth config is excluded only when the compatibility BLE surface is absent.
- User-visible names and User.long_name should remain LICHEN-branded, e.g. "LICHEN R1 Neo" or "LICHEN <board>".

Implementation status:
- Active Zephyr code enforces the core policy for firmware branding at both adapter local-info normalization and the public metadata encoder, uses PRIVATE_HW, CLIENT role, min_app_version 30200, and BLE/LoRa capability-derived fields.
- docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md was updated to make this policy explicit and replace the stale LICHEN_NODE reference with PRIVATE_HW (255).

Follow-ups/blockers created from review gaps:
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.23: Handle Meshtastic ADMIN_APP metadata read requests.
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.24: Verify current Meshtastic DeviceMetadata schema fields against Zephyr codec.
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.25: Align Meshtastic app-compat sync ordering vectors with adapter.
Existing related follow-up: project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.4 for peer enumeration/node DB sync.
notes:
2026-07-01 progress: claimed Bead, used two explorer subagents for schema/code acceptance context, verified upstream Meshtastic docs/releases by web check, updated docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md with explicit synthetic metadata/version policy, and created follow-up Beads project-LICHEN-t2hn.23, .24, .25 for untracked implementation/test gaps.

Review/fix evidence: Confucius found no initial blockers. Hubble found P1 that firmware branding was documented but not enforced for provider-supplied local_info; fixed by adapter firmware-version brand normalization and qemu test. Feynman found P1 excluded_modules=0 advertised all modules, P1 ADMIN_APP get_device_metadata_request not implemented, and P2 gateway firmware_version looked like a Zephyr kernel version. Fixed excluded_modules bitmask/doc/test and changed gateway firmware string to LICHEN compat 0.0.0+zephyr.x.y.z. Copernicus found P1 direct codec callers could still emit Meshtastic firmware strings; fixed in codec and added direct codec regression test. Leibniz found case-sensitive Meshtastic-brand rejection plus missing PRIVATE_HW/BLE-absent tests; fixed case-insensitive rejection and added assertions for DeviceMetadata/User hw_model plus excluded_modules 0x5fff/0x7fff. Halley noted closure blockers tracked as dependencies: project-LICHEN-t2hn.23 ADMIN_APP metadata reads, project-LICHEN-t2hn.24 schema verification, project-LICHEN-t2hn.25 sync ordering. Mencius/Einstein found Beads/code mismatch and LICHEN substring false positive; fixed durable Beads design and tightened code to require firmware_version to start with LICHEN token, reject meshtastic in any casing, reject NOTLICHEN, and allow valid LICHEN compat strings.

Verification: git diff --check PASS. west build -p always -b qemu_x86 lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -d build/meshtastic_adapter_qemu_t2hn15 && timeout-wrapped west build -d build/meshtastic_adapter_qemu_t2hn15 -t run PASS by PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL: meshtastic_adapter 20/20, RAM 184376 B / 31 MB, only Zephyr test RNG warning. west build -p always -b qemu_x86 lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec -d build/meshtastic_codec_qemu_t2hn15 && timeout-wrapped west build -d build/meshtastic_codec_qemu_t2hn15 -t run PASS by PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL: meshtastic_codec 15/15, RAM 180280 B / 31 MB, only Zephyr test RNG warning. Timeout wrapper terminates QEMU after success; no qemu-system-i386 processes remain. Pending final two-review pass, then commit and continue with blocking no-hardware Beads.

Additional review evidence: Jason found docs still needed stronger wording that firmware_version must start with an exact LICHEN token and must reject Meshtastic in any casing; fixed docs and tests. Averroes found P1 that rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/config_handler.rs still emits raw firmware_version and no excluded_modules. This file is not currently exported or compiled by rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/lib.rs, so the immediate compiled Zephyr policy is covered here, but closure is now blocked on existing follow-up project-LICHEN-t2hn.20 to wire or retire that Rust handler and enforce the same policy if it remains in the surface.
